question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause cache thrashing and repeated DB queries by sending requests for blocks whose account IDs cycle through values that exceed the LRU cache `MaxSize`, causing every `entityCache.Get(accountId.GetId())` call to miss and trigger a `GetAccountAlias` DB query, saturating the database?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user cause CPU and memory exhaustion by sending many concurrent `/block/transaction` requests with valid block identifiers, since each request calls `FindByIdentifier` and `FindByHashInBlock` (two DB queries) plus `updateOperationAccountAlias`, multiplying resource consumption per request?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/main.go -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the HTTP server to exhaust file descriptors by sending many concurrent requests that are never closed (slow-read attack), since the `http.Server` has a `WriteTimeout` but if it is set to 0 or a very large value from `rosettaConfig.Http.WriteTimeout`, connections accumulate until the OS file descriptor limit is reached?
